_comment: Demo 3 — Audio-LFO-Stripes. Skeleton for the audio-rate-LFO-as-spatial-banding demo. Requires source.mp4 (~10s plain dark gradient or solid color clip) + audio.wav (~10s dynamic instrumental: quiet intro, loud chorus, quiet outro). LFO operator running at 50Hz, modulated by audio RMS, bound to Y. Stripe density = LFO rate × frame timing.
name: Audio-LFO-Stripes
description: Sound becomes vision. A 50Hz LFO bound to Y produces ~50 horizontal stripes per frame — packing tight when music gets loud.

params._comment_rate: 50 Hz — high enough to produce ~50 stripes per 1080p frame at 60fps. LFO editor must allow audio-rate range (cap at 200 Hz). Bumping the cap is part of Creatrix PR-C.

mod_edges._comment: Audio RMS modulates LFO rate. Loud music → higher rate → tighter stripes. Quiet → wider stripes.
LFO output writes to hue.shift, bound to Y axis. The KEY paradigm unlock — LFO oscillation at audio rate becomes spatial banding because Y axis.

demoMetadata.label: Sound becomes vision. 50 Hz of LFO becomes 50 stripes per frame.
